reviews.review_title: did exact opposite of what its made for
reviews.review_description: this game markets itself as a way for you to learn the rules of the road and get more confident in your driving but from my experience with it as a recently licensed driver it only made me feel worse about my driving skill and made me want to be on the road less and on top of that even on the most sensitive steering setting you still have to turn the steering wheel for maybe five times to turn your wheels 45° most cars you only have to actually turn your irl steering wheel at most two times and on top of that the physics were absolutely terrible and the ai are constantly crashing in each other and being idiots making it physically impossible to actually follow the laws you're supposed to be obeying i recently got my driving permit and i am from indiana and i do not think that this app works as intended you would have better luck with a drifting simulator than you would this

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your experience and feedback. We've noted your comments and are continuously improving Road Ready VR through future updates. We appreciate you giving the game a try.

reviews.review_description: It’s actually pretty good graphics are not too bad for a young game. I would love weather cycle. Also horn. And adding the windshield feature. Also bigger map. And sometimes cars get stuck in traffic because of pedestrians standing in the road. And the mirrors need a little more work mainly the rear view. Also gas refill feature would be fun to get out and have to fill up car with gas and also being able to move outside car. Not trying to criticize the game, just trying to give ideas on how you can make it better and have more and more people playing.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the detailed feedback and suggestions, YvoXo! We really appreciate players taking the time to help us improve Road Ready VR.

Your feedback has been noted. Also, you can already use the horn by pressing the "A" button. 🚗📢

We're also excited to share that Walk Mode is coming very soon, and we're continuing to improve the maps, AI, mirrors, and overall driving experience with future updates.

Thanks again for supporting the game!

reviews.review_description: I love this game but can you add a horn to it so I can honk at the cars that hit me pls and thank you so much (edit) hey Mr melv here again and I just want to say thank you for adding the horn that I asked you and I play this game all most every day I feel like I'm actually driving an actual car and I know I'm asking for to much but can you make a air bag so when I crash it can blow if you cant then it's ok love (AS A FRIEND) 😉😉😉(edit) heyyy it's mrmelv onec again and I just have a question and I know I keep asking for to much now but can you make a 2019 Honda Civic LX in black if not then it's okay that's all I had to say and keep up the good work and I just can't stop playing this game its so fun and cool oh yeah and also can you add a windshield wiper that's all hey I'm here onec again when are you doing a update I just want to know umm you did not add the airbag (edit) ok idk now if you now but you think you can make a buffalo ny map if you can if not it's okay and can you sub to my YouTube channel liltinnypou I'm trying to get to 100 subscribers ❤️❤️❤️(edit) hey I seen you put the manual transmission in the game but can you put a test for 10 min pls I want to try it🥺🥺🥺 all so can you make the game not require Internet pls

developer_response.body:
Hey Mrmelv! Thanks again for all the support and for playing Road Ready VR so much 🚗❤️

We've noted all your suggestions. Manual Transmission Mode is now available, and we're continuing to work on new features and improvements. Thanks for being such a dedicated member of the community, and good luck with your YouTube channel! 😊

reviews.review_title: Terrible traffic AI. Do not buy
reviews.review_description: Spent $30 on this game with the advanced lessons and full license DLC. Terrible purchase, the traffic is unrealistic and really dumb, expect spending over 4 minutes at an intersection with only a few cars because one car is slightly misaligned. This game is a waste of money and I highly advice against purchasing any of its upsales

developer_response.body:
Hi camperdelarco,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We're sorry to hear that your experience didn't meet your expectations.

Regarding the traffic system, Road Ready VR is designed as a driving education simulator, and traffic behavior is intentionally governed by traffic laws, right-of-way rules, and vehicle positioning. That said, we understand that certain traffic situations can sometimes feel overly cautious or lead to longer-than-expected wait times, and we're actively working on improving AI traffic behavior and intersection flow in future updates.

We also take reports of vehicles becoming misaligned or causing traffic issues seriously. If you encountered a specific location or scenario where this occurred, we'd appreciate additional details so we can investigate and address it. Please reach out to us at admin@fakesouls.com and we will make this right for you.

Our goal is to continue improving the simulator based on player feedback, and we genuinely appreciate you giving Road Ready VR a try.

- The Road Ready VR Team

reviews.review_description: This game is one of the best driving games on VR, this game has lots of potential. I've been playing since it came out, not a doubt that i love it and enjoyed it. It would be so cool if you were to add more free cars. And that's all i have to say. Thank you for making this game. Recommended for all people reading, you will not be disappointed at all. God bless all.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words and support, eliecastinbackup! ❤️

It truly means a lot to hear that you've been enjoying Road Ready VR and see its potential. We're grateful to have players like you in our community. Your feedback about adding more free cars has been noted, and we're always looking for ways to make the experience even better.

Thank you again for playing, recommending the game to others, and taking the time to leave such a thoughtful review. Wishing you safe driving and many enjoyable miles ahead in Road Ready VR! 🚗

reviews.review_description: It’s a good app I think it needs a couple of updates like more signs and road types but the biggest issue for me was the rear view mirror you can make anything out also night time mode you can’t see anything in the mirrors really

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the helpful feedback, Hanniballectershiss! We’re glad you’re enjoying Road Ready VR overall.

We’ll definitely consider adding more signs and road types in future updates. We’re also aware of the rear-view mirror visibility issue, especially in night mode, and we’ll be working on improving that so the mirrors are clearer and more useful during driving. Thanks again for pointing it out.

reviews.review_description:
Positives - No motion sickness 
2, Seat adjustment is the best 
3, stepping out of the car to see the context and understand dimensions
4, Day and Night scenarios
5, Graphics compared to others till date
6, I want you to please incorporate 
Training programme like ( VR DRIVER SCHOOL) available in steam do 
7, I will be the first person to buy all ur upgrades if u do so. 

Negatives - 
1, Side and Back mirror not so practical not giving me the proper context. 
2, Road width is so less in free Roam 
3,In Basic training session. Instructions pop up is blocking my driver view 
4, Please add some more whether based scenarios. Rain, snow, 
5, let it be an Offline game 
Online only when there are updates required
6, Create some Hazard based scenarios

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this detailed feedback — this genuinely means a lot to us.

We’re really happy to hear you’re enjoying the comfort, seat adjustments, context view, day/night scenarios, and graphics. That’s exactly the experience we’re aiming for. And the VR Driver School idea? Noted. We love that direction.

We’re also taking your negatives seriously. Mirrors, road width in free roam, instruction pop-ups blocking view, weather scenarios, offline mode, and hazard-based training — all valuable points and already on our radar for improvements.

Appreciate you taking the time to break this down. Feedback like this helps us build Road Ready VR the right way.

- Fake Souls

reviews.review_description: This game is great, the turning is smooth, acceleration is nice, and braking feels good. Just needs some improvements. First, a good thing to add is a backup camera, because when I’m driving and charging my VR at the same time and I have a small charging cord, the charging cord will just snap off when I look back to reverse. Second, this game needs online multiplayer, because at one point I’m gonna get bored of playing by myself. Third, make sure the bots don’t break so fast in your face, like I keep crashing into them because of that reason, and I’m sure everyone did too, so make it so that they brake smoothly like us in the cars. Lastly, make a horn for us player, those bots be driving me crazy. So, if you add all of this you will make a great game.

developer_response.body:
Really appreciate the detailed feedback — this is super helpful. 🚗

Glad you’re enjoying the driving feel, that’s been a big focus for us.

Backup/rear camera → great point, especially for VR comfort.
Multiplayer → definitely something we’re thinking about long-term.
AI braking → noted, we’ll work on making traffic feel more natural.
Horn → love this idea, simple but adds a lot.

Thanks again for taking the time to write this — feedback like this genuinely helps shape the game.

reviews.review_title: This game is actually pretty good
reviews.review_description: This game is surprisingly fun and addictive especially if u pop on some Spotify and just drive around free roam. But I did wanna ask if its possible for u devs to add a police car to drive that would be cool and some sport cars

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the support, gymskin.0! We're glad you're enjoying Free Roam and cruising with your music on 😄🚗

Police vehicles and sports cars are definitely interesting ideas, and your feedback has been noted. We're always looking to expand Road Ready VR with new content and experiences. Thanks again for playing!

reviews.review_description: HORRIBLE GAME I TRIED TO BY A PACKAGE AND IT DIDN'T WORK WORST GAME EVER 😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭

developer_response.body: We're sorry to hear you're having trouble with your purchase. Please reach out to us at admin@fakesouls.com or join our Discord server for faster support, and we'll be happy to help investigate and resolve the issue as quickly as possible.

reviews.review_title: This is A Very good Driving Game
reviews.review_description:
Most Games I play In Vr are standing but I can have my chair and play this game

Right Now I’m Still trying to get all the controls to this game but This Is Like Very underrated!

You Should Buy this game Quick!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the support and recommendation, Capson2026! We're really happy you're enjoying the seated driving experience and finding Road Ready VR fun to play 🚗

If you're still learning the controls, be sure to try out the new Walk Mode as well. You can now get out of your vehicle and explore on foot in Free Roam.

Thanks again for being part of the Road Ready VR community!

reviews.review_description: Ok well this game is good and all but why is there police cars but they don’t even like give you a ticket at all and all they do is drive because isn’t this supposed to feel like your actually driving, and I also think you should let us walk around to like fill up gas or something idk but please let us walk around, and this is the last thing I think you should maybe fix, why do we need to pay for sooo much things like to get your full license and stuff, thanks

developer_response.body:
Hi Jen76, thanks for the feedback and suggestions.

We’ve noted your ideas about police cars, walking around, refueling, and more realistic interactions. We’ll keep these in mind as we continue improving Road Ready VR.

We also understand your concern about paid content and appreciate you sharing it.

Best regards,
Fake Souls

reviews.review_description: I like the game but, im aware the devs of this game may not be from the US but you can make a right turn on red and it failed me for doing so, also im barely a inch over the line and it fails me for entering on a red light. PLEASE FIX!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for reporting this. We recently fixed an issue related to right turns on red lights and traffic light detection. 

If you’re still experiencing this problem after updating, please reach out to us at admin@fakesouls.com and we’ll be happy to help investigate and resolve it.

reviews.review_title: Thing i think should be added
reviews.review_description: I think you should add opening doors rolling down windows and able to walk around when u even the car but its a fun game to play and can u please add reverse camera so when u reverse it shows what’s behind you

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the suggestions and support, Ablitorator2012! 

We’re glad you’re enjoying Road Ready VR. Features like opening doors, interactive interiors, walking around, and reverse cameras are all interesting ideas, and your feedback has definitely been noted as we continue improving the experience 🚗

reviews.review_description: Love the game. I would like to see different highway scenes as well as more roads. Other than that it’s an ok simulator. My son loves it and won’t get off 😆.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the support! We're happy to hear both you and your son are enjoying Road Ready VR 😄🚗

More highways, roads, and map expansions are already in the works, so stay tuned for future updates. Thanks again for playing and sharing your feedback!

reviews.review_description: Love this game please add: a horn, better Npc driving, jobs like taxi driver or doing deliveries, bigger map, whether options and more cars that are free.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the support! We’re glad you’re enjoying Road Ready VR 🚗

A lot of your suggestions like better NPC driving, bigger maps, weather systems, and more gameplay features are things we’re actively considering and working toward. Also, you can already honk using the “A” button 😄

reviews.review_description: After loading into any mode and riving for about 30 seconds i lose the ability to steer with my left hand, which wouldn't be a problem if i weren't left handed,

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback and for reporting this issue. We’re sorry to hear you ran into this steering problem, especially as a left-handed player. 

This definitely sounds unintended, and we’d love to investigate further. If possible, please share more details (device, game mode, steps to reproduce, or a video if available) with us at admin@fakesouls.com so we can look into it. We appreciate your support and helping us improve Road Ready VR! 🚗🙏

reviews.review_description: I like this game cause it’s realistic on driving terms

developer_response.body:
Hi Donald214,

Thank you so much for the 5-star review! We appreciate your feedback and are incredibly glad to hear you enjoy the realistic driving experience. Making the simulation feel true to life on the road is exactly what we aim for.

Drive safe, and thank you for playing!

reviews.review_title: Great tool for first time drivers
reviews.review_description: My son is learning to drive and this app is perfect for teaching him the situatuonal awareness required to drive on city streets.  There are other cars, pedestrians, stop signs and stop lights.  He’s excited about driving so getting him to practice on here was a no brainer.  Some of the controls are little wonky, but these little things can be patched.  Thank you for developing this crucial app.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your experience. We’re glad to hear Road Ready VR is helping your son build situational awareness and confidence in a safe environment—this is exactly what we set out to achieve.

We also appreciate the feedback on the controls and are actively refining them. Thank you for your trust and support.
-Team Fake Souls

reviews.review_description: I‘m very surprised, How everything works. I used on my Meta Quest 2 device. Would be very nice If the developer can add a camping or boat trailer for training reverse driving and parking at the camping site or harbour. Great software. Also for driving school training best choice.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the kind words and for trying Road Ready VR on Quest 2! We’re really glad to hear it’s working well for you. A trailer or camping reverse training scenario is actually a great idea and something we’ll definitely consider adding in the future. 

Our goal is to make Road Ready VR as useful as possible for real driving practice and training. Thanks again for the feedback and support!

reviews.review_description: Can you please add multiplayer so I can play the game with my friends?

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the suggestion! Multiplayer is something we'd love to add and it's planned as a feature later down the road as Road Ready VR continues to grow.

In the meantime, be sure to check out the new Walk Mode feature, which lets you get out of your vehicle and explore on foot in Free Roam. 🚗🚶

reviews.review_description: I LOVE THIS GAME IT'S SO FUN

developer_response.body:
Hi TJPCarr,

Thank you for the awesome review! We're thrilled to hear you're having so much fun with Road Ready VR. Feedback like yours motivates us to keep improving and adding new content.

Drive safe and enjoy the road! 🚗💨

- The Road Ready VR Team

reviews.review_description: I believe this game has a sky rocket of potential, I believe it would be great if we can customize cars or a new map. Love the game LATER DUUUDE

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the support! We really appreciate the feedback. Car customization and new maps are actually things we’re actively working on, so stay tuned for future updates. Glad you’re enjoying Road Ready VR 😄🚗

reviews.review_description: Its fun but if you run into cars pls make them chase you

developer_response.body: Thank you for the suggestion and support, Brannybrant! 😄 We’ve seen a lot of players ask for more advanced police and traffic interaction systems, so your feedback has definitely been noted as we continue expanding Road Ready VR 🚓

developer_response.body:
Thanks for reporting this, and sorry for the trouble. We’ve received reports from some users experiencing a black screen issue after the latest Quest system update.

Our team is actively working on a fix, and we’re aiming to roll out an update as soon as possible. Please stay tuned, and thank you for your patience while we get this resolved.

reviews.review_description: I love this training game is pretty cool. Driving the car and you can have accidents and no trouble from the police. This game is really cool especially for those who don't know how to drive they can practice with this game.

developer_response.body:
Hi KillShot28, 
Thank you so much for the awesome review. We’re glad you’re enjoying the experience and having fun learning with Road Ready VR. 
Helping new drivers practice and build confidence is exactly why we made the game. We really appreciate your support.

reviews.review_description: Y’all need to make a multiplayer driving game please.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the suggestion, Braeballer1! Multiplayer is on our roadmap, but it’s a bigger feature and likely further down the road. Right now, we’re focused on improving performance, adding lessons, cars, and customization first.

reviews.review_description:
I like this game and it teaches me
how to drive a almost car

developer_response.body:
Hi QuirkySphynx5271,

Thank you so much! We’re really glad Road Ready VR is helping you learn driving fundamentals and enjoy the experience. More updates are on the way, including Manual Transmission and other exciting features 🚗

reviews.review_description: Hey I'm unable to install this I have enough space and is connected to the internet

developer_response.body:
Sorry about that! Please try restarting your headset and installing Road Ready VR again. 

If it still fails, please reach out to us at admin@fakesouls.com with your headset model and any error message shown so we can investigate further.

reviews.review_description:
Grabbed this to practice the motion of parallel parking before my last few lessons and road test.

The camera doesn't reset properly, the rearview mirror camera isn't set to the right aspect ratio, NPC vehicles do not yield to the player or to traffic lights... i even got an advanced green for a left turn that doesn't exist.

This fails as an educational tool sadly.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed feedback — we appreciate you taking the time to share it.

Road Ready VR is currently in Early Access, and some systems you mentioned (camera behavior, mirrors, and AI traffic logic) are still being refined. Traffic rules and signaling are actively under development and not yet fully enforced in all scenarios.

If you’re willing, you can report specific issues in-game via Support → Report an Issue, which helps us address them faster. We’re sorry the experience fell short for you, and your feedback genuinely helps shape future updates.

reviews.review_description: Great game Supr realistic very nice for the fundamentals

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much! We’re really glad you’re enjoying the realistic driving experience and fundamentals training in Road Ready VR. 🚗

Thanks again and don't forget to share it with your friends and family!

reviews.review_description: Please make the tires more grippy and make it so the highway is just a straight line. Make the roads a little wider and the steering wheel is a little glitchy. Make the “night” map darker. I know these next few might not be possible but make the graphics better, add depth to the buttons and make the ai more human like driving. The game will be a lot better with these fixes. Sorry for all of the criticism but it is to make the game better

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share such detailed feedback — we genuinely appreciate it. Many of the points you mentioned, including steering behavior, road width, night map lighting, AI driving behavior, and overall visual depth, are already being actively worked on as part of upcoming updates.

Improving vehicle handling and realism is a top priority for us, and feedback like this helps guide those refinements. Please stay tuned — the next updates will focus heavily on polish, stability, and realism improvements. Thanks again for helping us make the experience better.

- Fake Souls

reviews.review_title: It can be a good app
reviews.review_description: but optimization is so poor that it's almost unplayable.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback, Fguillotine, and sorry the experience has been rough. We’re actively working on optimization and performance fixes to make Road Ready VR run smoother.

If possible, please report the issue from Main Menu > Support > Report an Issue and include your device model and what mode/lesson you were playing. That helps us track down the problem faster and prioritize the right fixes.

reviews.review_description: N9 n9

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We’re glad you found the game nice. We’re continuously improving the experience, so please feel free to share what would make it better for you.

reviews.review_description:
:)
i like it i don’t know about anyone else!?

developer_response.body:
Hi BrantleyDeSalvo7667,

Thank you so much for the 5-star review! We’re really happy to hear that you like Road Ready VR. Your support means a lot to us and helps us keep improving the game.

A new update is coming soon with more improvements and content, so stay tuned!

Best regards,
Fake Souls

reviews.review_description: It helps you learn how to drive

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, Keekee100.100! We’re really happy Road Ready VR is helping you learn and build confidence behind the wheel. More improvements and lessons are on the way!

reviews.review_description:
It’s a pretty good game overall it’s just the fact they don’t really have a variety of cars. You only have that one car so they could add a couple others like maybe some manuals and that would make it a lot better.
Because right now they don’t really have that much of a variety like you only start off with a Ford

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! More vehicle options and manual transmission are ideas we're exploring for future updates. Glad you're enjoying the simulator overall!

reviews.review_description: Can you guys please add a seatbelt and radio option where  we can take off and put on seatbelt and listen to the radio.

developer_response.body:
Hi SoraRose, Thank you so much for the 5-star review and for taking the time to share your ideas.

We really appreciate the suggestion. Adding a seatbelt feature is something we’ll include on our roadmap for future updates.

As for radio, adding real radio stations can be legally challenging due to licensing and streaming rights, but we’ll definitely look into creative ways to bring a radio-like feature or similar in-game audio experience to Road Ready VR in the future.

Feedback like this helps us shape the game, so thank you again for your support and for being part of the Road Ready VR community.

reviews.review_description: I wasn't looking for this game, nor did I know it existed. But the moment I saw it, I knew I had to download it and I'm so grateful I did. In the couple hours I spent playing it yesterday, I finally figured out aspects of driving that used to really confuse me that nobody could ever explain. I'm 31 years old and due to AuDHD and other issues, I struggle severely with learning and don't know how to do many things. This app is the perfect tool for me to learn driving in a safe environment entirely by myself. The things I was able to figure out on my own just by using this app have already helped me feel like driving, and my future independence, is actually within reach. I can't thank you enough for creating this game and I can't wait to see how it improves in future updates. I haven't tried the paid lessons yet, but I plan to once I'm comfortable moving past the free content. The only request I have is a larger city with more realistic traffic flow because learning to drive in city traffic is my biggest anxiety when it comes to driving. I did see in the app that you intend on adding scenarios and I really hope that includes avoiding various types of accidents.

developer_response.body:
Hi hb137mars,

This truly means a lot to us. Thank you for taking the time to share something so personal.

Knowing that Road Ready VR helped you understand driving in a way that finally “clicked” — especially after struggling for years — is exactly why we built this. Driving should feel possible, not overwhelming. If the app helped even a little in reducing that anxiety and building confidence, then we’re doing something right.

We also really appreciate you mentioning AuDHD and how learning differently can make traditional instruction harder. Creating a safe, self-paced environment where you can repeat, observe, and practice without pressure is one of our core goals.

Your feedback about a larger city and more realistic traffic flow is excellent. City driving anxiety is very real, and expanding urban scenarios — including accident-avoidance situations — is absolutely aligned with where we want to take this. We’re actively working on expanding infrastructure and more complex real-world scenarios in future updates.

Take your time with the free content. Build confidence at your own pace. When you feel ready, the advanced lessons will be there.

Thank you again for trusting us with something this important. Wishing you confidence, independence, and many smooth merges ahead.

— Fake Souls 🚗

reviews.review_title: Recommend these updates but great game
reviews.review_description: This game is honestly great and I like it but I just want to point out a couple updates y'all should do to make the game even better. First, y'all should add more maps to free roam, it gets kinda boring playing in the same map all the time. And lastly, it would be cool to be able to choose your own car and color, but y'all don't have to do that. These updates in my opinion can make the game better but either way this is still a good game and this is 5 stars without a doubt.

developer_response.body:
Hi XeMyles,

Thank you for the 5 stars and the thoughtful feedback — we really appreciate it.

More maps for Free Roam and car customization are great suggestions. Expanding environments and adding more personalization options are definitely things we’re considering as we continue to grow the game.

Thanks for taking the time to share your ideas and for supporting Road Ready VR!

reviews.review_description:
Who ever made this game doesn't realize how nice this game is 

Quality wise it's better then many dedicated driving games currently on VR. It has Nice graphics , cocktail view, working buttons and alive environments almost felt like GTA without the violence. 

 Imagine you turned this into a real racing game because you definitely got the foundations right

I'd also add you should implement working headlights lol when I turn them on there is no light coming from it or blinkers ...still a good concept for what it is.

developer_response.body:
Thank you, that really means a lot! 🙏
We’re glad the realism and cockpit view stood out—“GTA without the violence” is a compliment we’ll take 😄. Headlights and blinkers are noted, and more updates are definitely coming. Thanks for the encouragement and support!

reviews.review_description: I don't know who or what (AI) is building this app but this developer is going places. Somehow a random review by what I assume is a kid asking to be able to walk around seems to be taken seriously. You can walk around and even punch people now. Like what the GTA is going on here. Very trippy walking around but likely great as therapeutic for people who can't walk.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the support and kind words, Ghost781! 😄 We're glad you're enjoying the new Walk Mode and the freedom it brings to Road Ready VR. It has been amazing seeing community suggestions turn into real features.

There's plenty more planned for the future, and we can't wait to keep expanding the experience. Thanks for being part of the journey! 🚗

reviews.review_description:
I know it is an early access game but it is great the parking is good and the steering: you can get used to . There are some bugs though the traffic doesn't stop to allow you to park and just ram into you and they dont indicate . Also you need to add a manual mode and a radio 📻.  Overall it is a well designed game and hope you continue to update 😀 👍🏻  

Update: since the newest update i have encountered some problems. 1 the steering wheel is very odd it seem more realistic im the older updates . 2 The gearstick works partly but I'd glitches quite often (you can not put it in to drive or the gearstick stayes In neutral) there are a peculiar amount of bugs i have found but the game does deserve a five star rating (excellent for new umpire drivers. I would gladly request this to any new driver. I would leave a review about the add-on but can not afford it.

developer_response.body:
Hi Berrythree,

Thank you for the detailed feedback and for the kind words about the game. We’re glad to hear you’re enjoying the parking and overall design.

We’ve noted the issues you mentioned regarding traffic behavior, steering feel, and the gearstick glitches. Your feedback is very helpful, and we’ll take it into consideration for future updates as we continue improving the experience.

Thanks again for the support and for recommending the game to new drivers.  😊👍

reviews.review_description: I am not a licensed driver yet so I decided to try this game out to help my fear of driving and to see how it is. It is very good and the lessons were really helpful and I wish there were more for free! I like the detail in and outside of the car. But I would like it if the map, npcs, and other cars had just as much detail. A weather option would also be nice and an option to turn traffic and npcs off, and a custom radion to play what you want!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the thoughtful feedback, blzrds! We're really happy to hear that Road Ready VR has been helping with your fear of driving and that you've found the lessons useful. 🚗

Your suggestions for more free content, improved map and AI detail, weather options, traffic controls, and custom radio have all been noted. We're continuously working to make the experience even better. Thanks again for playing and sharing your ideas!

reviews.review_description: The first thing is, I feel the scale of vr is a little bit large. Just like driving a tailer. Seems if descale to 0.8-0.9 would be better. Second, image in the mirror deformed. Other advice is, environment can be much richer, Quest3 can handle higher resolution and frame rate. Holding up the hand on vr steering too long is quite tired.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the kind words and detailed feedback — we’re glad you’re enjoying Road Ready VR.

Great points on world scale, mirror distortion, richer environments for Quest 3, and steering comfort. All are noted and actively being evaluated for future updates.

Your feedback truly helps us improve. Thanks for playing and supporting the game!

reviews.review_description:
Road Ready VR is an excellent driving simulation designed to help players practice real-world driving skills in VR. You’ll train through a wide range of scenarios, including obstacle navigation, stop signs, parking maneuvers, and proper use of left and right indicators.

The standout feature is the free-roaming level, where you share the road with other cars and pedestrians. This really helps build situational awareness and gives a strong sense of realism. The car physics feel convincing, making each action—braking, steering, and turning—feel meaningful and grounded.

What makes this even more impressive is that the experience is completely free. It’s a very complete package and feels more like a true VR driving simulator than a simple game. I highly recommend it, especially for anyone preparing to pass their driving licence or wanting to build confidence behind the wheel in a safe virtual environment.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the detailed and thoughtful review! 🚗
We’re really glad the realism, free-roam mode, and driving physics stood out for you—that’s exactly the experience we’re aiming for. Appreciate the support, and we’ve got more improvements and updates coming soon!

reviews.review_description: Don’t download. You are unable to turn while accelerating and causes headaches. Yes - it does look enticing but it is not; this game is poor quality and not a good way to learn to drive, nor practice driving.

developer_response.body:
Sorry you had a frustrating experience. You should definitely be able to steer while accelerating, so that doesn’t sound right. Could you let us know which lesson or scenario this happened in and what device you’re using? That will help us reproduce and fix it quickly. 

You can also report details through Support → Report an Issue in the app. We appreciate the feedback and are actively improving the driving physics and comfort.

reviews.review_description:
this game has great potential, good physical but i wanna put somethings that need to be added or fixed

1. Fix the reset wheel.
so for the 1st car like the default car it works good like when ur not holding the wheel and it resets to it’s original position it works well for that car, but the other cars it glitches it steers like crazy it glitches for all the last 3 of the cars.

2. Make it to where u can get out the car and walk around.
i think it would be cool if we could do that

3. Fix npc cars
i hate everytime when im driving i get brake checked and it makes me crash into them.

4. add npc into cars
basically the npc cars and u can see a led driving

5. make us to where we can customise out avatar
i think it would be cool for us to customise ourself and while in the car u could see ur arms and legs and also have animated pedal leg so when u hit the gas ur legs is animated like it looks like it’s actually hitting the gas.

6. put some more details into the map. if it has more details it would look excellent 

7. a gas script 
it would be so realistic if we had a gas script it would be very nicer have it 

Honestly this is a good game, but some things need to be fix and added. overall this is a great game to play when u want to drive in vr. i love this game a lot i just got it like a day or 2 ago and i’ve been playing it often, i love to drive but at my age i cant drive and whenever i want to drive this is the game i go to!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the detailed feedback and suggestions, yokeys! 

We’re really glad you’re enjoying the game and appreciate you taking the time to share this. We’ve noted your feedback and will do our best to cover and improve on these in future updates.

Thanks again for supporting the game and helping us improve it! 🚗💙

reviews.review_title: Good game , here are thing you should ad
reviews.review_description:
So you should have a mode where you can drive along other players , with a day and night cycle going . On that mode the people 
should be able to work at gas stations , mcdonalds , stuff like this. Also , they will earn money so they can buy better cars , and even houses ,because if night comes you will have to sleep , bc if days go by without sleeping you will faint or sum . And to top it of ( make it easier to get to your job ,house ) add a map on the screen that shows your job ,house ,and stores so you can get food . Did I mention you have to eat and stay hydrated. Pls listen to me and your game will improve , I helped create a game on roblox ( Brookhaven like) that was once popular and had like almost 1 billion visits

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the detailed suggestions and support! We appreciate you taking the time to share your ideas. Features like multiplayer-style experiences, day/night systems, jobs, progression, and expanded gameplay are definitely interesting ideas and some are things we’ve discussed internally as Road Ready VR continues to grow.

For now, our focus is on improving the core driving experience, maps, AI, and upcoming major features, but your feedback has absolutely been noted. Thanks again for supporting the game 🚗

reviews.review_title: Fix hand recognition on merging act 5
reviews.review_description: I was onto merging lanes with moving vehicles and on the third merge , I couldn't grab the signal lever and it wouldn't register i was turning left and I failed 10 times , so I gave up, because it wasn't registering I was signaling left,

developer_response.body:
Hi Faithkr,

Thank you for the detailed feedback — and I’m really sorry you had to fail multiple times because the signal didn’t register. That’s frustrating, especially during a merge with moving traffic.

We’re going to investigate and fix this as soon as possible. Could you please confirm the exact lesson name where this happened (was it Highway Merge (Advaned) or another lesson)? Also, did the signal lever move visually, or did it not respond at all?

If you notice anything else, please use Support > Report an Issue from the main menu and share as much detail as possible (lesson name, what step you were on, left/right hand used). That helps us reproduce and fix things much faster.

We appreciate you taking the time to report this — we’ll work on getting it resolved quickly.

— Fake Souls

reviews.review_title: As a game on beta it’s pretty good
reviews.review_description:
First of all ill list out all the problems: The npc’s are pretty dumb  they switch lanes right in front of you causing you to crash which shows the poor script then when in traffic they manage to brake instantly before touching the other car which is pretty unrealistic, no honk, no manual cars only automatic, very difficult to control the car as it tends to slide much when going over 80+, the steering wheel doesn’t go back to normal on it’s own when left and need to turn i back yourself,  civilians are invincible and cause you to crash into them without them moving a inch which is pretty unrealistic, poor map really basic. Thing you should do: fix all mentioned before,  maybe a police system where if u crash or drive recklessly they might chase or pull over which would start a conversation with the officer throught the windows which  you should add  a Button do lower it, maybe upgrade the map make it more realistic  and a actual city  with  skyscrapers light led, make more car options, install an automatic blinker removal which every car has in real life, make the steering wheel harder to turn , make an option to put either night or day so if someone enjoy the dark vibes they can.
This should be all i hope i mentioned everything i needed to say i hope the developers will take these tips and make the game better, as i said i know this game is only on beta, so its pretty obvious there’s  some bugs. Bye
-the great reviewer

developer_response.body:
Hi Davix_buxin, 

Thank you for the detailed feedback and suggestions — we genuinely appreciate you taking the time to write all of this out.

A few things you mentioned are already in the game:
- You can honk using the “A” button
- Steering sensitivity can be adjusted in Car Settings
- Day / Evening / Night can be selected before entering Free Roam
- Automatic blinkers turn off after wider steering turns
- Steering wheel centering when you let your hands off the steering wheel.

The rest of your feedback has been noted as well. We’re continuously working on improving Road Ready VR and expanding the experience over time, especially while the game is still in beta. Thanks again for supporting the game and sharing your ideas 🚗

reviews.review_description: I know this is a wip game, however there are some things that should be a priority, driving on this game is fun until your car decides to drift and all you can do is over correct into a wall, this usually happens in the highway and the wheel makes no sense, I mean in how you hold it, also hands position to the real world is wrong, and having the wheel not center by itself is a challenge and also the physics of grabbing the wheel and steering it is just garbage, please get these core functions working, I’ll update my review once this gets addressed, I’ve recently seen the UI change and it was better than before honestly.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the detailed feedback. You’re right—steering behavior, wheel centering, hand positioning, and grab physics are core systems and a priority for us. We’re actively working on improving these to better reflect real-world driving.

We appreciate you acknowledging the recent UI update, and thank you for your patience as Road Ready VR continues to evolve.

— Fake Souls Team

developer_response.body:
Thank you! 🚗
We’re really glad it feels close to real-world driving — that’s exactly what we’re aiming for.

More realistic features and improvements are on the way!

reviews.review_description:
I loved the night city vibes, the experience was lag free and smooth. The 😭 cars do crash into you so you gotta learn how to drive defensively. I noticed that motion sickness doesn't get triggered when you are sitting on the floor while driving. You can also crash into pedestrians btw 🤣👌🏻 

I love the quality that is being delivered in the game given its free

developer_response.body:
Thank you! We’re really glad you enjoyed the night city vibes and smooth performance 😊

The defensive driving aspect is very much intentional, and it’s great to hear it came through naturally. We also appreciate the motion-sickness note — comfort and accessibility are big priorities for us as we keep improving the experience.

Thanks for the support and for giving Road Ready VR a try!

Fake Souls

developer_response.body:
Hi kenpop41,

Sorry to hear you didn’t have a good experience. Road Ready VR focuses more on realistic driving practice than arcade-style graphics, but if something wasn’t working as instructed, that’s not okay.

Could you let us know which lesson or mode this happened in? You can also use Support > Report an Issue so we can look into it quickly.

reviews.review_description: I know that is a brand new game, so this is a great start, here are my problems: The steering is a bit problematic with the controllers, and the gearshift lever is sometimes buggy, preventing it from moving up and down. All in all, it's a good start.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the honest feedback — and for seeing the potential.

You’re spot on: steering feel and the gear shifter reliability are known Early Access issues, and both are actively being worked on. Input consistency and interaction stability are high-priority improvements for us.

We really appreciate you taking the time to call these out. Feedback like this helps us tighten the experience faster.

reviews.review_description: Amazing game it is helping me learn to drive. Also I like it from first person because it simulates real driving.

developer_response.body: Thank you! We're glad Road Ready VR is helping you learn to drive. Our goal is to make the experience as realistic and useful as possible.

We’re actively adding more content, including improved highway scenarios and new features to keep things engaging longer. More updates are on the way. 🚗

Wishing you the best on the road!

reviews.review_description:
I can drive but I wanted to see what this was like.
It's very realistic.
Indicators...parking...stopping and starting at lights and junctions.
Usable mirrors.
It's great I just drive round for fun ☺️ 

I do find the steering with 2 controllers a bit hard 
so I end up just using 1.
Might be better with some kind of wheel attachment 

10/10 developers ..great job 👍

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much! 😊 We’re really happy you’re enjoying the realism — especially the mirrors, indicators, and junctions.

Great feedback on the steering too. We’re exploring ways to make it feel more natural, including wheel-style controls. We’re also working on more updates, so stay tuned! 🚗✨

Really appreciate the support — it means a lot to us! 🙏

reviews.review_description: I was looking for car games to play, until I came across this game, everything looked so cool, until I needed to grab the steering wheel with both controllers, I only had one working at the time and I really wanted to play but I couldn't, please make a setting where you can control the car with only one controller

One-controller support is a helpful suggestion, and we’ll consider adding a setting for it in upcoming updates so the game can be more accessible for players with different setups. We appreciate you bringing this to our attention.

Multiplayer and additional maps are great ideas and definitely things we’ve discussed internally. Right now, we’re focused on expanding the core driving experience and adding new environments step by step. Your feedback helps us prioritize what players want most, so thank you for sharing it!

reviews.review_description: Sorry but with are the camera controls, i never seen controls that bad for a vr POV. For how many times u put the car in drive and u accidentally shut off the car gets annoying fast

developer_response.body:
Thanks for trying the game and for the honest feedback. Sorry the camera controls and drive/shut-off flow felt frustrating — that’s definitely not the experience we want.

Could you let us know what camera/control setup you’d prefer to see? We’re actively reviewing player feedback and will look into making this smoother in future updates.

reviews.review_description: Great simulation

developer_response.body: Thanks for the support! We're happy you're enjoying the simulation and are continuing to improve it with new features.

reviews.review_description: This game is in early access, so it's understandable for the controls to feel slightly janky. I was having fun learning how to parallel park, but one of my issues is that the steering wheel sometimes doesn't always follow where I rotate my controller. I hope in the future that I'll see better controller tracking. Another improvement that I'd like to see is hand placement on the wheel, since the game forces you to grip the wheel on the sides which prevents me from practicing the 'push and pull' technique while turning. So summarising what I said, it would be great if the Devs were to allow us to place our hand anywhere on the wheel instead of forcing them to be on the sides.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the thoughtful and detailed feedback — we really appreciate you taking the time.

You’re absolutely right on both points. Steering-wheel tracking consistency and more flexible hand placement are active areas we’re improving, especially to better support real-world techniques like push-pull steering. Early Access feedback like this directly shapes our roadmap.

Glad to hear parallel parking was still useful, and thanks for helping us make Road Ready VR better with such practical insights.

reviews.review_title: good game but could use improvements
reviews.review_description: this is a very fun and enjoyable game but a idea would be to add a car selector, stick shift,and multi player.Again very good game

developer_response.body:
Hi CondieFamJam,

Thank you for the kind words — we’re really glad you’re enjoying the game!

A car selector and stick shift are great suggestions. We’re continuously expanding features, and feedback like this helps shape future updates.

If you have more ideas, feel free to use the “Report” button in the main menu and share them with us. We appreciate your support!

reviews.review_title: Learn how to drive a car simulator
reviews.review_description:
This is great. 

I already can drive a car, but I think this is a good practice for those who are still learning.
Starting with the practical lessons and then in the real traffic.

Before starting a lesson, you can put your seat in the right position.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the detailed feedback! We’re glad you see the value both for new drivers and as a practice tool. Starting with lessons and building confidence before real traffic is exactly what we’re aiming for. More improvements are on the way!

reviews.review_description: Its ok. Fun, but once you finish all the basic lessons you now have to pay for the premium ones. I feel there are better driving games out there.

The core lessons are free so players can learn the fundamentals, while premium lessons cover more advanced real-world scenarios and ongoing development.

We’re continuing to expand the free content as well — more to come.
